microsoft_ft: Owned end-to-end program delivery for Azure's recovery validation program, scaling a **2-person operation** into a platformized system sustaining 45+ annual resilience drills and driving **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that **reduced operational toil by 30%** and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model aligned to contractual delivery timelines.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability — delivering a rack-level drill program in **4 months** with a 94% recovery rate — surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation model across hardware and software systems.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, coordinating engineering, legal, contracting, and finance stakeholders to meet all requirements.

Embedded directly with enterprise customer teams to translate mission-critical recovery objectives into engineering sprint plans and execution milestones, ensuring technical outcomes aligned to **contractual requirements** and customer-defined success criteria.

Scaled team leverage through AI-driven automation, building an internal AI agent and restructuring planning workflows to **reduce planning cycle time by 39%** and increase drill capacity by 21%, enabling the program to grow without proportional headcount.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of **AI-driven code generation** workflows across 14 Azure service teams, conducting user demos and training sessions that drove sustained utilization and saved **37 engineering hours monthly** through improved developer tooling integration.

Shaped the product roadmap by facilitating **11+ structured user interviews** with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing prioritization of intent-based YAML generation as a core capability for the next development sprint.

Optimized data integrity and retrieval by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool, cutting engineer lookup time by 83% and improving cross-team knowledge accessibility at scale.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework, coordinating execution across 140+ engineering and operations teams toward shared delivery milestones.

Built a product strategy to eliminate operational toil during region launches, conducting discovery with **20+ service teams** to quantify 81 hours of manual effort per region and delivering a roadmap to automate critical deployment paths for hardware and software systems.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to track and visualize operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps, allocate resources to high-impact workstreams, and make data-driven prioritization decisions during complex deployments.

Coordinated with engineering, finance, and technical operations stakeholders to validate automation impact projections, building executive-facing program reports that translated complex multi-team execution data into **clear milestone tracking** and go/no-go decision frameworks.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by owning the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping hardware and software dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and sequencing rollout milestones to de-risk integration across the production floor.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning, backlog prioritization, and retrospectives across a multi-disciplinary engineering team, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and sustain execution velocity through a complex system migration.

Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering teams to deploy automated CI/CD pipelines, **accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25%** and reducing manual integration steps for hardware-software system updates across the robotics fleet.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by owning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a new CRM workflow that standardized execution and enabled the team to scale throughput without adding headcount.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversion rate by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the company website and Google profile to improve inbound lead quality and reduce cost-per-booking.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records to identify margin leaks, delivering actionable findings that informed revised estimating standards and protected gross margin on future contracts.

skills_priority: AI agents, workflow automation, process optimization
tailoring_notes: 'Contractual requirements' woven into microsoft_ft bullet#4 — mirrors JD language about meeting all contractual requirements as a core TPM responsibility.

'Hardware and software systems' integration language used in microsoft_ft bullet#3 and amazon_robotics bullet#1 — directly mirrors JD requirement for deployment and integration of sophisticated hardware and software systems.
'Sprint plans' and 'execution milestones' used in microsoft_ft bullet#5 — mirrors JD's explicit call to build sprint plans and execution plans with clients and engineers.

'Cross-functional alignment' and multi-stakeholder coordination (engineering, finance, legal, contracting, technical operations) mirrored in microsoft_ft bullet#4 and microsoft_2022 bullet#3 — reflects JD's emphasis on orchestrating nearly every internal team.

'Embed with customer teams' framing in microsoft_ft bullet#5 mirrors JD's Embed/Engage section calling for working side-by-side with customers to translate mission challenges into engineering outcomes.
